task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock main application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigated to the Portfolio Management view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Portfolio Management' tab is selected and active in the JStock application.
An 'Import' dialog, file chooser, or CSV mapping window is open within JStock.

task_completion.description: Selected the correct CSV file for import
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'import_portfolio.csv' in the Documents folder is selected in the file chooser, or its contents/path are visible in the import dialog.

task_completion.description: Portfolio successfully imported with correct positions
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Portfolio Management view displays the imported stocks with the correct units: TSLA (30), META (40), JPM (60), V (20), and JNJ (45).

integrity.description: Agent used the GUI to import the portfolio, not terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing commands like 'cp' or 'mv' directly modifying the buyportfolio.csv file in the hidden .jstock directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the JStock application
integrity.visual_evidence: The portfolio is viewed and managed inside the JStock application interface, not just opened in a text editor or spreadsheet software like LibreOffice Calc.

integrity.description: Agent used the import functionality rather than manual entry
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the import dialogs being used, rather than the agent manually clicking 'Buy' and typing in the stock tickers and quantities one by one.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target import file is located at ~/Documents/import_portfolio.csv. The expected final portfolio positions in JStock are: TSLA (30 units), META (40 units), JPM (60 units), V (20 units), and JNJ (45 units).
